rulings.Case Title: VALERIE LEE VS. MICHAEL W. SPENCER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To For Mandatory And Discretionary Relief
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, June 9, 2014, line 6. DEFENDANTS MICHAEL A. SPENCER and MICHAEL W. SPENCER's Motion For Mandatory And Discretionary Relief DENIED. Mandatory relief is not available as there was no default judgment. Even if such relief was available, Michael A. Spencer failed to appear in Court on May 19, 2014: (1) Michael A. Spencer was purportedly advised that trial call was at 9:30 a.m.; (2) as of the time of the entry of judgment after the court trial at 11:48 a.m. Michael A. Spencer failed to appear. There is no evidence presented that Michael W. Spencer intended to appear; or made any requests to be excused from appearance prior to the trial call. Contrary to Defendants' assertions no such request appears in Michael W. Spencer's Declaration is support of Motion for Summary Judgment filed on March 27, 2014. Also, on May 15, 2014 Defendants requested continuance of trial. Request was based solely on counsel's unavailability through July 25, 2014. Request to continue UD trial on ex-parte basis for 10 weeks was denied. Discretionary relief is denied. No declarations from either defendant have been submitted to the Court. Court judgment for possession and damages is not subject to CCP 1169 and is not void. = (501/REQ)
